Project Greyharbor — Heads of Terms for the recommended acquisition of Shoreminster Payment Services Ltd by North Quay Holdings plc (UK issuer).
Listing/Regulatory: North Quay to remain on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange; UK MAR and the F C A Disclosure Guidance apply; target is an authorised payment institution.

Financing: Rookfell Credit Fund I LP to underwrite £110,000,000 at SONIA + 325 bps; equity placing of c.£90,000,000; Long Stop Date 30 September 2026; reverse break fee £3,200,000 if shareholder vote fails.

must_detect.matched_text:
Ms. Lila Pembridge (CEO) to sign a service agreement and 12‑month non‑compete; contact e‑maiI l.pembr!dge@shoreminster-pay.co.uk; LTIP awards to comply with the FCA Remuneration Code (SYSC 19D).
Special-category data (circulated to Clean Team only): Ms. Pembridge has Type 1 diabetes requiring an insulin pump; her biometric facial‑scan template (Ref FAC‑7x9q—UK) is used for facility access; she is a member of Dockside Finance Union and identifies as bisexual; she volunteers for the Harbor Reform Party and practises Buddhism; her son is a minor aged 16; explicit consent captured on 03/05/2026.
Data protection: processing under UK GDPR Art. 9(2)(a)/(b), restricted to U K  storage; data are pseudonymised; retention for 6 months post‑Completion; access strictly need‑to‑know per Annex A Clean Team protocol.
Financing: Rookfell Credit Fund I LP to underwrite £110,000,000 at SONIA + 325 bps; equity placing of c.£90,000,000; Long Stop Date 30 September 2026; reverse break fee £3,200,000 if shareholder vote fails.
Structure and timetable: Scheme of Arrangement under Part 26 Companies Act 2006; irrevocable undertakings secured from holders of c.38%; registrar Oakhaven Registrars Ltd; expected Court sanction within 90 days.
Public‑source references and generic materials: Companies House filings, the UK listing rules handbook, and a standard DEI policy; product names mentioned (OrbitOS, NebulaCRM) are illustrative; Kendry Town FC will host the family day at Darnell‑on‑Sea.
Governance and law: governing law England and Wales; exclusive LCIA arbitration in London; not an offer to the public under FSMA s.21; not a prospectus; not a Material Adverse Change representation or warranty.
Integration: TUPE consultation for Shoreminster Ops; reasonable adjustments to continue, including quiet space for prayer; for clarity, this sentence refers to health and sexual orientation only in general policy terms and not to any named individual.
Contact and portals: Mr. Jonah Vale, Corporate Development, North Quay Holdings plc, +44 20 3889 4455, jonah.vale@northquayplc.co.uk; dealroom https://greyharbor.example/ts?v=1#id=UK‑TS‑9z4b.
Miscellany: fees, costs, and exclusivity binding; confidentiality applies to all “inside information” under UK MAR; document watermarks may appear mis‑scanned.
Obfuscated identifiers: Deal Ref NQH‑TS—0O1l; Target Co Reg No. 1l8  093  772

must_detect.matched_text:
Project Greyharbor — Heads of Terms for the recommended acquisition of Shoreminster Payment Services Ltd by North Quay Holdings plc (UK issuer).
Listing/Regulatory: North Quay to remain on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange; UK MAR and the F C A Disclosure Guidance apply; target is an authorised payment institution.
Consideration: £188,000,000 cash at £4.70 per Shoreminster share; funded via equity placing and a committed term facility; completion mechanics per City Code timetable.
Conditions precedent: approval of shareholders by ordinary resolution; change in control clearance under FSMA s.178; no CMA intervention; for the avoidance of doubt, no MAC clause shall apply before Completion.
Market abuse controls: recipients are wall-crossed; this term sheet contains precise, non‑public information likely to significantly affect price; maintain insider lists and do not deal until the Clean Period.
Management & employment: Ms. Lila Pembridge (CEO) to sign a service agreement and 12‑month non‑compete; contact e‑maiI l.pembr!dge@shoreminster-pay.co.uk; LTIP awards to comply with the FCA Remuneration Code (SYSC 19D).
Special-category data (circulated to Clean Team only): Ms. Pembridge has Type 1 diabetes requiring an insulin pump; her biometric facial‑scan template (Ref FAC‑7x9q—UK) is used for facility access; she is a member of Dockside Finance Union and identifies as bisexual; she volunteers for the Harbor Reform Party and practises Buddhism; her son is a minor aged 16; explicit consent captured on 03/05/2026.
Data protection: processing under UK GDPR Art. 9(2)(a)/(b), restricted to U K  storage; data are pseudonymised; retention for 6 months post‑Completion; access strictly need‑to‑know per Annex A Clean Team protocol.
Financing: Rookfell Credit Fund I LP to underwrite £110,000,000 at SONIA + 325 bps; equity placing of c.£90,000,000; Long Stop Date 30 September 2026; reverse break fee £3,200,000 if shareholder vote fails.
Structure and timetable: Scheme of Arrangement under Part 26 Companies Act 2006; irrevocable undertakings secured from holders of c.38%; registrar Oakhaven Registrars Ltd; expected Court sanction within 90 days.
Public‑source references and generic materials: Companies House filings, the UK listing rules handbook, and a standard DEI policy; product names mentioned (OrbitOS, NebulaCRM) are illustrative; Kendry Town FC will host the family day at Darnell‑on‑Sea.
Governance and law: governing law England and Wales; exclusive LCIA arbitration in London; not an offer to the public under FSMA s.21; not a prospectus; not a Material Adverse Change representation or warranty.
Integration: TUPE consultation for Shoreminster Ops; reasonable adjustments to continue, including quiet space for prayer; for clarity, this sentence refers to health and sexual orientation only in general policy terms and not to any named individual.
Contact and portals: Mr. Jonah Vale, Corporate Development, North Quay Holdings plc, +44 20 3889 4455, jonah.vale@northquayplc.co.uk; dealroom https://greyharbor.example/ts?v=1#id=UK‑TS‑9z4b.
Miscellany: fees, costs, and exclusivity binding; confidentiality applies to all “inside information” under UK MAR; document watermarks may appear mis‑scanned.
